authoritative_sources.title: Hospital Inpatient Quality Reporting Program
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS/QualityNet program source for hospital inpatient quality reporting; authoritative for hospital inpatient measure, submission, public-reporting, and payment-update context that constrains hospital analytics claims.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Inpatient quality metric definition and public-reporting posture

authoritative_sources.title: Hospital Outpatient Quality Reporting Program
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS/QualityNet program source for hospital outpatient quality reporting; authoritative for outpatient measure, facility, submission, and public-reporting context before comparing hospital outpatient performance.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Outpatient quality metric definition and facility-reporting context

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Necessary Requirement
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official HHS/OCR HIPAA guidance is authoritative for limiting protected health information in hospital analytics outputs, service-line examples, low-volume drilldowns, disclosure packets, and escalations to the minimum necessary for the stated purpose.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: PHI handling, audience review, and disclosure minimization

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Claims Processing Manual
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS claims-processing manuals are authoritative for Medicare hospital billing, inpatient and outpatient claim workflow, CMS-1450/837I context, claim-state semantics, and payer/provider operational evidence used in encounter-to-claim reconciliation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: ADT, encounter, charge, claim, remittance, denial, and appeal validation

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Interoperability and Prior Authorization Final Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS prior-authorization guidance is authoritative for prior-authorization process, documentation, payer response, and patient-access workflow dependencies that can gate hospital service analytics interpretation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Eligibility, referral, authorization, and payer-dependency checks

authoritative_sources.title: United States Core Data for Interoperability
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the National Coordinator for Health Information Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official ONC interoperability baseline is authoritative for healthcare data classes and terminology used to trace patient, encounter, problem, procedure, medication, laboratory, and clinical-operational evidence in analytics.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Healthcare data lineage and terminology normalization

authoritative_sources.title: HL7 FHIR US Core Implementation Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HL7 US Core is the recognized implementation guide for US FHIR profiles used to represent patient, encounter, condition, procedure, observation, and related clinical-operational data lineage without substituting for clinical interpretation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Standards-based hospital data lineage and terminology mapping

authoritative_sources.title: eCQI Resource Center
authoritative_sources.publisher: Assistant Secretary for Technology Policy / eCQI Resource Center
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official federal eCQI resource is authoritative for eCQM specifications, QRDA implementation guidance, value sets, and standards-based quality-measure evidence handling.

authoritative_sources.title: AHRQ Quality Indicators
authoritative_sources.publisher: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AHRQ Quality Indicators are recognized public hospital quality and patient-safety measure resources; authoritative for using inpatient administrative data indicators only with explicit specification, denominator, risk, and caveat handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Hospital quality indicator and patient-safety measure handling

authoritative_sources.title: NHSN Patient Safety Component
authoritative_sources.publisher: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CDC NHSN is the public national surveillance framework for healthcare-associated infection and patient-safety reporting; authoritative for HAI terminology, facility reporting context, denominator caution, and escalation when surveillance interpretation is required.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Patient-safety surveillance and HAI terminology handling

authoritative_sources.title: HCAHPS Quality Assurance Guidelines
authoritative_sources.publisher: Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services / HCAHPS
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The CMS HCAHPS quality-assurance guidance is authoritative for hospital patient-experience survey administration, reporting, and caveats before using patient-experience results in health-system analytics.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Patient-experience metric and disclosure caveat handling

authoritative_sources.title: Provider Data Catalog and Care Compare
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS public provider-data platform is authoritative for public-reporting posture, provider-data display context, dataset lineage, and external disclosure caution when internal hospital metrics are compared with public performance data.

workflow_stages.name: Tenant, audience, facility, patient/member, and reporting-context intake
workflow_stages.description: Confirm jurisdiction, business unit, facility, campus, department, service line, payer, plan, provider type, care setting, reporting period, patient/member status, intended audience, disclosure posture, public-reporting posture, and source-precedence rules before analysis starts.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Prerequisite and authorization dependency validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate eligibility, referral, authorization, service date, coverage, consent, admission status, discharge documentation, care-transition records, order documentation, and medical-record dependencies when they affect access, throughput, utilization, claim, denial, or operational metrics.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Metric definition and measure logic control
workflow_stages.description: Define numerator, denominator, exclusions, facility and service-line attribution, cohort, refresh window, source tables, measure references, action thresholds, comparison baseline, and publication thresholds before calculation or narrative synthesis.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Source collection, reconciliation, and confidence scoring
workflow_stages.description: Collect and reconcile ADT, bed-management, scheduling, EHR, EDW, order, discharge, referral, authorization, charge, claim, remittance, denial, appeal, quality, utilization-review, and BI records while preserving source conflicts and lag assumptions.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Analysis package assembly and caveat drafting
workflow_stages.description: Assemble metric specifications, analytical summaries, caveats, action thresholds, confidence limits, source-lineage notes, exception registers, and recommended next questions for the approved audience and cadence.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Privacy, disclosure, and adjacent-authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Stop when PHI handling, minimum-necessary posture, public reporting, clinical judgment, patient safety, payer interpretation, coding policy, legal interpretation, quality attestation, staffing or bed-management approval, or operational approval is required.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

artifacts: tenant policy, jurisdiction, business unit, facility, campus, department, service line, payer, plan, provider type, facility type, care setting, and reporting-audience records
patient or member identity, eligibility, admission status, encounter status, service dates, consent, referral, authorization, order, discharge, care-transition, and documentation dependency records
ADT events, bed-management records, encounter, scheduling, referral, authorization, charge, claim, remittance, denial, appeal, and utilization-review extracts

EHR or EMR reporting, enterprise data warehouse, governed BI semantic model, practice-management, revenue-cycle reporting, payer portal, quality reporting, patient-experience, case-management, and scheduling analytics outputs

quality and patient-safety measure specifications, numerator and denominator logic, exclusions, value sets, eCQM, QRDA, HCAHPS, NHSN, AHRQ Quality Indicators, benchmark, and public-reporting references
data-quality exception logs, source-reconciliation notes, metric dictionaries, refresh logs, lineage maps, disclosure-risk notes, action-threshold notes, and escalation packets
systems_of_record: enterprise data warehouse and governed BI semantic layer
EHR or EMR reporting, ADT, bed-management, order, discharge, care-transition, and clinical documentation systems
practice-management, scheduling, patient access, referral, and authorization platforms
revenue-cycle, charge-capture, coding, clearinghouse, claims, remittance, denial, appeal, and payment posting systems
quality reporting, eCQM, QRDA, HCAHPS, NHSN, registry, utilization review, case-management, and public-reporting platforms
payer portal, plan, eligibility, benefits, coverage, referral, and authorization data stores
privacy, consent, disclosure, compliance, and approval workflow systems

allowed: classify the request as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing hospital prerequisites

choose the authoritative metric definition, source hierarchy, denominator, numerator, exclusions, facility and service-line attribution, source tables, refresh window, action threshold, and cadence for descriptive hospital analytics
determine whether data quality, denominator logic, lineage, confidence, and disclosure posture are good enough to publish an internal analytical result
prepare metric specifications, analytical summaries, caveat notes, data-quality exception registers, reconciliation notes, disclosure-risk notes, and next-evidence request lists
escalate: required tenant facts, facility context, patient/member status, payer, plan, care setting, source hierarchy, eligibility, referral, authorization, consent, admission status, documentation prerequisite, or retrieval hooks are missing and would change the answer
public guidance, payer policy, tenant policy, measure specifications, or system records materially conflict
PHI handling, privacy obligation, minimum-necessary analysis, low-volume disclosure, patient/member safety, clinical risk, patient-safety surveillance, or public-reporting posture changes the answer
clinical, payer-policy, coding, medical-necessity, legal, compliance, quality attestation, revenue-cycle approval, staffing, bed-management, or operational leadership authority is required
refuse: publish or imply certainty when source quality, signoff, definitions, denominator logic, or disclosure authority is unresolved
diagnose, plan treatment, determine medical necessity, make clinical judgments, or substitute analytics for licensed review
reinterpret payer policy, coverage rules, coding policy, appeal strategy, privacy law, or regulatory obligations
state unsupported causal, public-facing, regulatory, benchmark, staffing, bed-management, safety, patient-outcome, or policy conclusions
disclose PHI or identifiable examples without minimum-necessary and approval context

domain_failure_modes: treating health system analytics as generic BI and failing to request facility, campus, service line, payer, plan, provider type, care setting, patient/member status, source hierarchy, or privacy context

collapsing ADT, bed-management, encounter, order, discharge, charge, claim, remittance, denial, appeal, referral, authorization, quality, and utilization-review records into one truth set without explaining lags and ownership

publishing hospital quality, patient-safety, access, throughput, or utilization metrics without explicit numerator, denominator, exclusions, measure source, cohort logic, refresh window, benchmark posture, and public-reporting posture
inferring eligibility, referral, authorization, payer coverage, inpatient status, medical necessity, coding correctness, or denial appeal outcomes from incomplete administrative data
using PHI, low-volume cohorts, patient/member examples, clinician-level drilldowns, or service-line outliers without minimum-necessary, consent, audience, and approval controls
turning descriptive trends into unsupported causal, clinical, patient-safety, payer-policy, compliance, staffing, bed-management, or public-performance conclusions

letting the analytics lane widen into licensed clinical judgment, privacy-law interpretation, coding policy, quality attestation, regulatory submission, hospital accreditation attestation, or public disclosure approval

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
Metric definition is ambiguous or disputed.
Source data is incomplete, stale, or conflicting.
Stakeholder asks for a claim or disclosure not supported by the data.
Different systems disagree on the metric outcome.
A stakeholder asks for stronger conclusions than the data supports.
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes.
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist.
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing.
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis.
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific hospital metric dictionaries and payer-specific operating rules
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Hospitals and health systems differ on access, throughput, occupancy, length-of-stay, discharge, denial, utilization, patient-experience, quality, and patient-safety KPI definitions by facility, campus, service line, payer, product, care setting, and contract. The package treats these as runtime prerequisites and requires public or tenant baseline plus explicit tenant override before publishing.

State law, payer contracts, accreditation programs, facility policy, and tenant policy can be stricter than federal public guidance. The package escalates when local privacy, disclosure, clinical-risk, patient-safety, or reporting authority changes the answer.

notes: Authoritative hospital analytics records are the tenant-designated EHR or EMR, ADT, bed-management, EDW, governed BI, practice-management, revenue-cycle, scheduling, referral, authorization, claim, remittance, denial, appeal, utilization-review, quality-reporting, patient-experience, payer, privacy, and disclosure systems constrained by the cited public guidance.

The lane stops before diagnosis, treatment planning, medical necessity, payer-policy interpretation, coding-policy interpretation, privacy-law interpretation, quality attestation, Hospital IQR/OQR/eCQM/HCAHPS/NHSN submission, public disclosure approval, regulatory submission, staffing approval, bed-management approval, and patient-safety judgment.
